description: Subscribing to an allowed root key includes events for that root and all of its readable descendants.
A subscription only yields descendant events that the user is allowed to read.
The list of subscribable root keys should be predefined by configuration rather than accepted as arbitrary subtree keys.
Clients should be able to subscribe to a document root but not to an arbitrary section or nested subtree inside that root.
Subscribing to a root key outside the predefined allowed set should return `invalid`.
When a client WebSocket command cannot be accepted, the server should return a `status` message with the relevant `OperationStatus` and optional `message`.
When a client WebSocket command includes `id`, the matching acknowledgement or `status` response should echo that same `id`.
Configured subscribable roots should not overlap. This should be treated as a configuration warning or validation error rather than a runtime merge rule.
Repeated `subscribe` messages extend the active root-key set for the same connection.
Duplicate root keys are normalized without error.
When duplicate root keys are received the most recent subscription entry wins.
Unsubscribing a root key that is not currently subscribed is a no-op and does not raise an error.
A valid `unsubscribe` command should return `unsubscribed` even when some requested root keys were not active.
When a user unregisters or the connection closes all active subscriptions tied to that user or connection are removed.
